claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: all(sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then phi(n) = n - 1.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or (phi(n) == n - 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) = n / 2.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (phi(n) == n // 2) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then phi(n) = (p - 1) * (q - 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (phi(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then the number of divisors of n is odd.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (tau(n) % 2 == 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power p^k (k >= 1), then phi(n) = n - n / p.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 1) or (phi(n) == n - n // min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is odd.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then the sum of the divisors of n is (p + 1) * (q + 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (sigma(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then the sum of the divisors of n is n + 1.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or (sigma(n) == n + 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then the sum of the divisors of n is 2n - 1.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (sigma(n) == 2 * n - 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power p^k (k >= 1), then the sum of the divisors of n is (p^(k + 1) - 1) / (p - 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 1) or (sigma(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) ** (vp(n, min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) + 1) - 1) // (min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then the sum of the divisors of n is odd.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (sigma(n) % 2 == 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then n divides (p - 1) * (q - 1) * (p + q).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (n % ((min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]))) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then the sum of the divisors of n is congruent to 1 modulo p.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 1) or (sigma(n) % min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) == 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then n divides phi(n) + sigma(n).
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or (n % (phi(n) + sigma(n)) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then n divides phi(n) + sigma(n) + 1.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (n % (phi(n) + sigma(n) + 1) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then n divides phi(n) * sigma(n).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (n % (phi(n) * sigma(n)) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power p^k (k >= 1), then n divides phi(n) * sigma(n) - (p - 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 1) or (n % (phi(n) * sigma(n) - (min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1)) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then the product of the divisors of n is n^(tau(n)/2).
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (prod(d for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n ** (tau(n) / 2)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then n^2 divides (phi(n) + sigma(n))^2 - (n + 1)^2.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or ((phi(n) + sigma(n)) ** 2 - (n + 1) ** 2) % (n ** 2) == 0 for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then n^2 divides (phi(n) + sigma(n) + 1)^2 - (2 * n)^2.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then n^2 divides (phi(n) * sigma(n))^2 - ((p - 1) * (q - 1) * (p + 1) * (q + 1)).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or ((phi(n) * sigma(n)) ** 2 - ((min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1))) % (n ** 2) == 0 for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power p^k (k >= 1), then n^2 divides (phi(n) * sigma(n) - (p - 1))^2 - (p^(2k) - p^(k + 1) + p^(k - 1)).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 1) or ((phi(n) * sigma(n) - (min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1)) ** 2 - (min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) ** (2 * vp(n, min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]))) - min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) ** (vp(n, min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) + 1) + min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) ** (vp(n, min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) - 1))) % (n ** 2) == 0 for n in range(1, 501))
